Title: Khaled says government to assess new opposition leader Samsuri
Article:
MUAR: The government will monitor the performance of Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar in his new role as opposition leader, Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said.
It was too early to evaluate Samsuri as the appointment was only announced yesterday, he said.
He said the government would observe and assess performance in all positions, including the opposition leader.
It was too early to evaluate
Samsuri as the appointment
had only just been announced, he said.
Samsuri, who is Perikatan Nasional (PN) chairman and Terengganu menteri besar, will replace Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin as opposition leader.
Hamzah was
sacked from Bersatu
in February.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rafizi, Nik Nazmi take over Parti Bersama Malaysia
Article:
PETALING JAYA: Former economy minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and former natural resources and environmental sustainability minister Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad today took over Parti Bersama Malaysia.
The party, also known as the Malaysian United Party, was established in 2016.
It was founded and helmed by its president, Tan Gin Theam.
Also present were several members of parliament, namely Wong Chen (Subang), Datuk Bakhtiar Wan Chik (Balik Pulau), Rodziah Ismail (Ampang), Zahir Hassan (Wangsa Maju) and Lee Chean Chung (Petaling Jaya).
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pua questions PH-BN pact after Johor seat move
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: DAP's Tony Pua has urged Pakatan Harapan (PH) to begin planning an exit from the unity government, warning that the bloc risks being "stabbed in the back" by Umno.
The former Damansara member of parliament said Umno's decision to contest all 56 Johor state seats in the upcoming state election had exposed the party's "true colours".
"By declaring their intent to contest all 56 state seats in Johor in the upcoming state elections, Umno has revealed its true colours, wanting its cake and eating it too.
"This makes an electoral pact between Harapan and Barisan Nasional in GE16 completely untenable.
"It is time to set a timeline to dissolve the Madani coalition, lest we be stabbed in the back by the treacherous Umno," he said in a Facebook post.
He said PH had done everything possible to maintain political stability under the Unity government, despite what he described as an "awkward arrangement" with BN following the 2022 general election.
He said sacrifices had been made in the interest of stability, which he acknowledged had benefited the country.
However, he said the arrangement with Umno should not be taken for granted, adding that there was little reason to trust assurances from Umno leaders that Johor BN's move would not affect federal ties.
"They cannot be trusted," he said.
He said PH must be prepared for the possibility of contesting future elections independently, despite potential challenges and negative voter perceptions.
He said it would be better for PH to lose with dignity than to continue facing what he described as political betrayal.
Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi yesterday announced that BN would contest all
56 state seats
in the upcoming Johor election, a move widely seen as an early indication of BN's intention to strengthen its position ahead of GE16.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nearly 60pct of Malaysian workers overweight or obese
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Nearly 60 per cent of Malaysian workers who underwent health screenings were found to be overweight or obese, revealing a worrying health trend in the workforce.
The findings were based on an analysis of the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) Health Screening Programme (HSP) from 2023 to 2025.
Deputy Human Resources Minister Datuk Khairul Firdaus Akbar Khan said the 59.2 per cent figure was significantly high and could not be taken lightly.
"The data also showed that about 19 per cent of them suffer from diabetes, while 17.45 per cent have high blood pressure.
"59.24 per cent of those screened have high cholesterol, with this risk profile concentrated among workers aged between 40 and 59," he said.
He said this during the launch of the Perkeso Run and Ride 2026 in conjunction with Labour Day at Dataran Wawasan today.
Also present were Human Resources Ministry deputy secretary-general (policy and international) and acting secretary-general Dr Mohd Shaharin Umar, Youth and Sports Ministry secretary-general Datuk Rahimi Ismail, Human Resources Ministry deputy secretary-general (operations) Sutekno Ahmad Belon, Perkeso chairman Datuk Seri Subahan Kamal and Perkeso group chief executive officer Datuk Seri Dr Mohammed Azman Aziz Mohammed.
Khairul Firdaus said the public must realise that the pre-retirement phase is a highly productive yet critical period for non-communicable disease (NCD) risks.
"In fact, the Health Ministry has revealed that only 14.7 per cent of senior citizens in Malaysia currently achieve healthy ageing," he said.
He also expressed concern over cancer, which remains the third-highest cause of death in Malaysia and is becoming increasingly common among younger people.
He urged the public to take the dangers of NCDs seriously, using their participation in the event as an intervention toward a healthy lifestyle and balanced nutrition.
"I am proud of the positive momentum shown today, as participants in their 40s and 50s represent nearly 30 per cent of the total turnout for the Perkeso Run and Ride 2026.
"This is a very good development, and I hope all workers, regardless of age, will continue to practise a healthy lifestyle.
"The health of our workers determines our productivity and directly influences our socioeconomic sustainability," he said.
The ninth edition of the event drew its largest turnout yet with 12,000 participants.
This year's edition expanded the running categories to 3km, 6km and 12km to encourage active participation, alongside the traditional 35km fun ride.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Proton strengthens market presence in Sabah, Sarawak
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Proton Holdings Bhd has strengthened its position in Sabah and Sarawak, with sales volume rising 283 per cent since 2018.
The national carmaker said sales in Sabah and Sarawak increased from 7,697 units in 2018 to 29,461 units in 2025, driven by sustained demand for models such as the Proton Saga, Proton X50 and Proton X70.
Market share in both states also grew over the same period, rising from 10 per cent to 21 per cent, reflecting stronger brand acceptance and an expanding customer base.
Meanwhile, Proton launched its 10,000 sMILEs Expedition, a cross-border convoy covering Sabah, Sarawak and Brunei as part of its efforts to further strengthen engagement with customers in Sabah and Sarawak.
The expedition, flagged off in Kota Kinabalu on May 16, is held in conjunction with Visit Malaysia 2026 and the Kaamatan festive season.
It involves a convoy of Proton vehicles travelling through major cities, rural towns, coastal highways and highland routes across Borneo.
Proton said the expedition reflects its commitment to staying closely connected with the people of Sabah and Sarawak, not only through its vehicles.
It also highlighted its continued investments in ownership experience, aftersales support and long-term community engagement across Sabah and Sarawak.
"Today, Proton grew its extensive network of 26 sales and service outlets across Sabah and Sarawak, alongside two outlets in Brunei, supported by a dedicated Regional Parts Centre that strengthens parts availability and improves service efficiency across the states.
"The expedition will also feature multiple dealership and service touchpoints throughout the route, reinforcing Proton's focus on ownership confidence and accessibility for customers across urban and rural areas alike," it said.
Proton deputy chief executive officer Datuk Ir Abdul Rashid Musa said the company's responsibility as a national automotive brand goes beyond building vehicles, extending instead to building meaningful relationships with the communities that have supported it over the years.
He said Sabah and Sarawak are home to generations of loyal Proton customers, and the 10,000 sMILEs Expedition reflects the company's appreciation for their continued support.
Abdul Rashid said the journey aims to strengthen ties with the people of Sabah and Sarawak, demonstrate how its vehicles perform in real-world conditions, and build trust through improved products.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: A day after BN, Johor PH says it will contest all 56 seats too
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Johor Pakatan Harapan (PH) today announced it will contest all 56 state seats in the upcoming state election.
State PH chairman Aminolhuda Hassan said the decision follows a declaration yesterday by Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi that his coalition would contest all state seats.
"The Johor menteri besar has stated that the state BN will contest 56 seats, so Johor PH will also contest all 56 seats. They have made their position clear," Aminolhuda said.
"Although some have questioned why we would contest against our own allies, it is because they made the declaration, so we are responding," he added.
He said this when speaking on behalf of Johor PH delegates at the PH 2026 Convention at the Persada Johor International Convention Centre here today.
Aminolhuda, who is also Johor Amanah chairman, said PH was confident of defeating BN for the second time, noting that the coalition previously lost the state during the 14th General Election.
"If we could win in 2018, we can defeat them in the next election," the Sri Gading member of parliament said.
Yesterday, Onn Hafiz had said the decision to contest all 56 state seats was made after taking into account the views of Johoreans and grassroots leaders from state Umno and BN on the party's direction.
In the 2022 Johor polls, BN won 40 state seats, PH took 12, Perikatan Nasional won three, and Muda secured one.
The convention is seen as a key platform in determining the coalition's direction, including preparations for potential state elections in Johor and Melaka this year.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UK seeks greater trade, investment growth with Malaysia: High Commissioner
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The United Kingdom (UK) aims to increase its trade with Malaysia this year after recording an 11 per cent growth in 2025, bringing the total bilateral trade to 6.5 billion pounds (RM34.26 billion), said the British High Commissioner to Malaysia Ajay Sharma.
"Last year, our trade grew by 11 per cent, and I am really keen for us to do even more together (this year) to benefit our people.
"The UK and Malaysia have a really strong, historic and multi-dimensional relationship. And it's based on trade, education and people-to-people links, " he told Bernama when met here, recently.
He said beyond trade, the UK is also looking to expand its cooperation with Malaysia in investment, both in the traditional and new sectors such as technology, semiconductors and the green energy transition.
The High Commissioner said Malaysia has a very strong semiconductor sector and the UK is keen to deepen collaboration in the sector.
Sharma also revealed plans to encourage more British companies to use Malaysia as a hub for Asean operations and increase investments in the country.
"You (Malaysia) have a skilled workforce. You have a really strong advanced manufacturing industry base. We (British) really want to take advantage of that. And Asean is a growing market. I also want us to get involved in attracting more investment from Malaysia into the UK," he said.
Sharma said he is also looking forward to deepening cooperation with Malaysia in Islamic finance and the defence industry.
"We are part of the Five Power Defence Arrangements. On the industrial side, we have worked very well together in the past, for example with the Hawk fighter jets.
"What I am looking at now is how the UK and Malaysia can collaborate on a defence industrial project that will bind our two countries together for decades.
"For example, this could involve the development of fighter aircraft, particularly through the potential sale of Eurofighter Typhoon aircraft to Malaysia. I see that as a stepping stone for closer cooperation between our two countries," he said.
Sharma added that the UK also hopes to work more closely with Malaysia in education and talent development, particularly in technical and vocational education and training (TVET).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Foreign-linked funding claims against Mandiri: Let authorities investigate, says Fahmi
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Allegations involving purported foreign-linked funding connected to youth-based non-governmental organisation Mandiri should be left to the authorities to investigate and verify, Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said today.
The PKR information chief, who was responding to allegations raised by the party's youth wing, Angkatan Muda Keadilan (AMK), said the government would allow enforcement agencies to determine the authenticity of claims circulating online.
Fahmi added that a police report is believed to have been lodged, though he could not confirm further details.
"You may refer to the police regarding the report," he told reporters today.
The controversy surfaced after AMK called for an investigation into claims that foreign funds were allegedly channelled to some people and organisations for political activities, including cyber operations and mobilisation efforts aimed at destabilising the government.
Amid the growing political storm, Mandiri denied allegations linking the organisation to foreign interference or any plot to topple Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
In a statement, Mandiri executive director Amir Hadi said the organisation was
considering legal action
against a group that allegedly made defamatory accusations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Chinese national arrested for trespass, alleged disruption at KLIA Terminal 1 [WATCH]
Article:
SEPANG: Police have arrested a Chinese national for trespassing into a restricted security area at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) Terminal 1.
The woman is also believed to have acted aggressively towards security personnel after entering the restricted area without a flight ticket, police said.
KLIA police chief Assistant Commissioner M. Ravi said the incident occurred at 1.35pm on May 16 at the international departure gate C of Terminal 1.
He said the woman had passed through the autogate security checkpoint despite not having a flight ticket before becoming aggressive when approached by security staff for inspection.
The suspect was detained by Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd auxiliary police at 1.45pm the same day, he said in a statement today.
Ravi said investigations found the suspect, the woman, had entered Malaysia on April 30 for a holiday with a friend.
"However, she did not have sufficient money to purchase a return ticket to her home country," he said.
He added that the woman was brought before the Sepang Magistrate's Court today and remanded for further investigation under Section 7 of the Protected Areas and Protected Places Act 1959.
The offence carries a fine of up to RM1,000, imprisonment of up to two years, or both upon conviction.
View on Threads
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Big risks and rewards in upcoming IPOs at SpaceX, OpenAI, Anthropic
Article:
NEW YORK: Wall Street is licking its chops over an unprecedented slate of massive initial public offerings (IPOs) set to arrive in the coming months, beginning with Elon Musk's SpaceX in June.
That is expected to be followed by artificial intelligence rivals OpenAI and Anthropic. The trio of mega listings, each eyeing valuations around US$1 trillion or more, constitutes a heady period of elevated risk and reward.
SpaceX is targeting an initial public offering that would raise up to US$80 billion, roughly double the funds generated from all 2025 IPOs.
OpenAI and Anthropic are eyeing IPOs raising US$60 billion, also huge numbers compared with the norm.
"We're really in unprecedented times," said Emily Zheng, an analyst for PitchBook, a research platform specialising in private capital. "And this concentration is more extreme than ever."
The trio is poised to enter public markets as the Middle East war adds to inflationary pressures and fogs the geopolitical landscape.
But that factor is not expected to impede the arrival of SpaceX, OpenAI and Anthropic.
"These three companies are kind of unique," said Jay Ritter, a specialist in IPOs at the University of Florida.
Mark Roberts, managing partner at the Blueshirt Group, also expects the offerings to be well subscribed.
"There's enough capital to enthusiastically embrace these three companies if they are priced correctly," Roberts said.
Nasdaq, where SpaceX will trade, announced earlier this spring that it would speed up the timeframe for including such mega listings in its main benchmark index.
The shift is expected to prod additional stock purchases of SpaceX from investment funds built around the index.
REWARD OR RECKONING?
Among portfolio managers for larger funds, SpaceX "is probably viewed as a must-have stock," said Roberts.
In anticipation of the listings, there has been a throng of activity on secondary markets where investors are buying unlisted securities, pushing Anthropic's theoretic value to more than US$1 trillion.
Both OpenAI and Anthropic have warned investors against securities not authorised by the companies.
Once they begin trading on public markets, their performance will serve as a gauge of the market's appetite for additional offerings, particularly in the AI market.
"If these companies do really well -- especially the AI ones, like OpenAI and Anthropic -- it would be a confirmation of these really massive private-market valuations," Zheng said.
"But the opposite could also be true," she added. "If the companies don't perform well, investors might conclude they're overvalued."
Some investors who have backed the three heavyweights in private markets are poised to cash out, potentially positioning them for the next round of tech companies.
Private equity firms currently hold more than 30,000 companies that they hope to exit, a backlog that has slowed availability of capital for new prospects. A recent Wall Street Journal article highlighted the slowdown, citing one firm that called the dynamic a "winter of exits."
A poor performance by the new entrants could hit the valuations of these private companies, Zheng said.
By going public, the companies will also subject themselves to greater scrutiny from investors.
The market will "be laser-focused on the performance of those stocks from an operational perspective," Roberts said. "So they can't miss their earnings."
Ritter predicted all three companies could see volatility.
"There's going to be big upswings and big downswings, because nobody knows the future," he said. "Owning these stocks is not for the faint of heart."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: DayOne plans dual IPO in Singapore and US, FT reports
Article:
HONG KONG: Global data centre operator DayOne, affiliated with China's GDS Holdings, is planning to list simultaneously in Singapore and the US in a move that is set to raise US$5 billion, the Financial Times reported on Sunday.
Reuters could not immediately confirm the report. DayOne did not immediately respond to a request for comment.
DayOne initially considered a sole listing in New York, but has been persuaded by Singaporean stock market officials to co-list, the FT report said, citing three people familiar with the plans.
Reuters reported in February that DayOne planned to raise US$5 billion in a US initial public offering, citing a source with knowledge of the matter, adding the company could be valued at US$20 billion in the IPO.
Shanghai-based GDS Holdings set up GDS International in Singapore in 2022, which was rebranded as DayOne in January 2025 following its separation from the parent company.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Senior policeman arrested over alleged extortion in Bukit Bintang
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A senior police officer has been remanded for four days to assist in an investigation into the alleged extortion of a man in Bukit Bintang earlier this year.
It is believed that the suspect holds the rank of Assistant Superintendent.
Dang Wangi police chief Assistant Commissioner Sazalee Adam confirmed the arrest and said the case was being investigated under Section 384 of the Penal Code for extortion.
He said the suspect was remanded for four days beginning May 16 to assist in investigations.
The alleged incident occurred at 1.30am on March 14 at a restaurant in Jalan Angsoka, Bukit Bintang.
Sazalee said the complainant claimed his colleague was detained by a group of policemen after exiting a car.
The colleague was allegedly detained on suspicion of involvement in scam or call centre activities.
The alleged incident took place at about 1.30am on March 14 at a restaurant along Jalan Angsoka, Bukit Bintang.
The group also claimed the complainant's employer, a Chinese citizen, was wanted by authorities in China and Cambodia, Sazalee said.
The complainant later met his colleague and found he had been handcuffed for nearly an hour before being released.
"He claimed he was forced to hand over RM5,000 to the individuals involved at the restaurant," Sazalee said.
Later the same day, at 10pm, the complainant allegedly received a phone call asking him to meet at a premises in Ampang to discuss settling the case involving his employer.
"The complainant alleged that he was again asked to hand over RM2,500 during the meeting," he said.
Sazalee said the complainant later lodged a police report as he was dissatisfied with the actions of the policeman involved, including allegedly handcuffing his colleague and threatening him for money.
He added that investigations into the case were ongoing.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Raja Muda of Perlis leads list of 24 state award recipients
Article:
ARAU: The Raja Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Faizuddin Putra Jamalullail, led a list of 24 recipients of state awards, honours, and medals at the first session of the investiture ceremony today.
The ceremony was held in conjunction with the 83rd birthday celebration of the Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail, at Istana Arau.
The Perlis state government said in a statement that Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin consented to confer the Darjah Kebesaran Seri Setia Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail (SSSJ), which carries the title Datuk Seri Diraja, on Tuanku Syed Faizuddin Putra.
The Ruler also consented to confer the honours on Raja Puan Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Dr Hajah Lailatul Shahreen Akashah Khalil and Tengku Puteri Mahkota of Perlis, Datuk Seri Datin Paduka Hajah Sharifah Fazira Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail.
The investiture was held at the Blue Room of Istana Arau.
Meanwhile, the statement said Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar was among 21 recipients of state honours at the ceremony held at the Balairung Seri of Istana Arau.
Shamsul Azri was awarded the Darjah Seri Paduka Mahkota Perlis (SPMP), which carries the title Datuk Seri.
Others conferred the honour include Armed Forces chief General Datuk Malek Razak Sulaiman, Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail, Public Service director-general Tan Sri Wan Ahmad Dahlan Abdul Aziz, former Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Razarudin Husain, and MIGHT co-chairman Tan Sri Dr Ahmad Tajuddin Ali.
The statement said the Darjah Datuk Setia Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail (DSSJ), which carries the title Datuk Paduka, was conferred on royal family member Tengku Ariez Hazaril Tengku Datuk Paduka Abdul Halim and PPK Teknologi Sdn Bhd chief executive officer Dr Aminuddin Datuk Paduka Ahmad Kayani.
It added that Inland Revenue Board deputy director-general (tax operations) Shaharrudy Othman received the Darjah DatukPaduka Mahkota Perlis (DPMP), which carries the title Datuk.
In addition, the Darjah Datuk Setia Paduka Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail (DSPJ), which carries the title Datuk, was awarded to the army 5th Division Commander Major General Shamshor Jaafar, army headquarters Operations and Training (OPLAT) director Major General Hashim Osman, Health Ministry doctor Jasmeet Singh Saren, Firefly Airlines captain Wan Muzairul Wan Mahazir, and Malaysian Institute of Accountants (MIA) president Saniza Said.
Meanwhile, the Darjah Datuk Panglima Sirajuddin Jamalullail (DPSJ), which carries the title Datuk, was conferred on  KPJ Perlis Specialist Hospital medical director Dr Hari Ram Ramayya, Universiti Sains Malaysia (USM) legal advisor Dr Khairul Anuar Che Azmi, Konsortium Bumita Nepta Sdn Bhd managing director Lee Mo Toi, and Tan Swee Leong.
The statement said the first session of the investiture concluded with the conferment of the Darjah Setia Mahkota Perlis (SMP) on Training Management, Army Training and Doctrine Command (PLDTD) director Brigadier General Azhar Ahmad, Education Ministry Matriculation Division deputy director Nor Asnidar Aziz, and Perlis Amateur Athletics Association honorary secretary, Iyaru Muthusamy.
Raja Perempuan of Perlis, Tuanku Tengku Fauziah Almarhum Tengku Abdul Rashid, was also in attendance at the investiture.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BN-PH alliance still intact, says Fahmi, denying Johor BN's solo move would jeopardise ties
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Pakatan Harapan (PH) remained prepared for any political possibility, including growing speculation of an early Johor state election, while insisting cooperation between PH and Barisan Nasional (BN) in the federal unity government remained intact.
Speaking during the 2026 PH Convention, communications director Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the coalition is fully prepared for any political scenario amid intensifying speculation over an early Johor state election.
He also dismissed claims that BN Johor latest political manoeuvre signalled cracks in cooperation between the two coalitions.
"Johor PH had already conveyed its position following Johor BN's announcement that it intended to
contest all 56 state seats
in the next state election.
Fahmi, who is also PKR information chief, said the party would now await further direction from the coalition's top leadership.
Asked whether Johor BN's move could jeopardise PH-BN cooperation, Fahmi rejected the notion outright.
"No. It is not a sign that cooperation with PH will end," he said.
However, he acknowledged that BN's declaration could still open the door for negotiations between both coalitions ahead of the next Johor polls.
"Maybe, maybe," he said when asked whether BN's move suggested room for electoral discussions with PH.
Earlier,
Johor PH chairman Aminolhuda Hassan
said that BN's move to contest solo in the upcoming state election is a clear signal that the door to political cooperation with them is now closed.
The Sri Gading member of parliament said the announcement by Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi shows that BN/Umno is no longer interested in continuing cooperation with PH.
"When they say they want to contest all 56 seats, they are the ones rejecting cooperation. Even before the convention, Johor Amanah vice-president Suhaizan Kaiat had already raised the question of whether there would be BN-PH cooperation in Johor for the election.
"But before the convention, they had already issued such a statement, which means they themselves have shut the door. They have declared there will be no cooperation with PH," he said at the same event today.
Political speculation in Johor has escalated in recent days following talk of a
possible dissolution of the state assembly
, with June 24 widely circulated online as a possible date for a special sitting linked to a snap state election.
Fahmi said PH was unfazed by the growing rumours and stood ready for any eventuality.
"Pakatan Harapan is ready for any possibility," he said.
The convention itself reflected strong grassroots momentum within the coalition, with Fahmi describing turnout as "extraordinary."
"As of midday, registrations have exceeded 5,000, while total attendance is estimated at more than 8,000.
"Some attendees did not register, some are outside the hall area, but the turnout has been truly extraordinary and very encouraging," he said.
Fahmi said delegates from across the country, including youth and women representatives, had actively participated in debates and policy discussions ahead of keynote speeches by PH's top leadership later in the day.
He added that the gathering had proceeded without incident.
"So far there have been no worrying incidents. In terms of security, attendance and registration, everything has gone smoothly," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: A Proton Saga sparked a lifelong passion for local innovation
Article:
I was in Standard Six at Clifford Primary School, Kuala Kangsar, back in 1985, waiting for Mak to pick me up. It was already almost 6 pm, and there was no sign of her car.
The crowd thinned until only three of us were left. Panic started to set in, but I tried to stay calm, hoping she would appear at any moment.
One of the other boys suggested we just walk; his father was likely stuck at work.
The others agreed, and i felt I had no choice but to join them, or risk being left alone in the encroaching dark. By car, the trip took twenty minutes. On foot, it would take over an hour.
As we walked, a few passersby gave us concerned, questioning looks. I was worried and scared at first, but slowly, the walk turned into an adventure.
Then, mid-journey, i saw it: a parked green Proton Saga. It was the first national car, only recently launched by the Prime Minister. We had watched the unveiling on TV just a week prior.
I remember the surge of pride I felt, seeing the first car ever built by Malaysia. And there it was in the flesh. We jumped with excitement, completely forgetting our fear and exhaustion.
For that moment, the walk home was no longer something to endure; it became something memorable.
We reached home just before Maghrib. When I walked in, I found Mak in the kitchen heating up dinner.
The moment she saw me in my school uniform, her face went pale. She almost screamed, realising she had completely forgotten to pick me up.
She rushed to me, apologising and checking if I was okay, noting how tired and messy i looked.
I tried to stay calm for her sake, but I could not stop the tears from bursting out. Now that I was safe, the fear of the dark walk finally caught up to me.
Mak comforted me, urging me to shower, pray, and get ready for dinner. My brothers teased me mercilessly, but my younger sister looked at me with pity.
To them, I proudly shared my discovery: I had seen the Proton Saga with my own eyes.
A few years later, when computers began capturing attention in the late 80s, that same spark returned.
I read every article I could find, dreaming of owning one. When i finally got my first computer, i taught myself to code using books from the school library. Technology has stayed with me ever since.
I get excited by new technology, but my wife has noticed a pattern.
She says I am "too patriotic", that my excitement peaks when the innovation is Malaysian.
I think she is right. It brings back that same feeling my 12-year-old self had on that long walk home - a sense of possibility, even in the middle of uncertainty.
I have seen this play out many times. When a foreign solution has flaws, we call it a work in progress. When a local one does, we question its worth entirely.
We accept beta versions from others, but from ourselves, we expect the finished product.
That mindset does not build innovation; it quietly stops it from growing.
For some, finding a flaw becomes an opportunity to say, "I told you so."
But innovation does not grow in an environment of constant doubt. It grows when imperfections are seen as part of the journey, not the reason to abandon it.
Innovation is not a straight road. It is uncertain, uncomfortable, and rarely perfect from the start.
Sometimes, like that green Proton Saga by the roadside, it is not perfect but it is enough to make us believe in what it could be.
It is time we stop cheering for the failure of our own people, and start helping them get better.
* The writer is chief executive of the National Cyber Security Agency
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mindef sets up three committees after Norway cancels missile export licence
Article:
MUAR: The Defence Ministry has established three committees following Norway's cancellation of export licences for the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) anti-ship missile system to Malaysia.
Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the committees would focus on contractual decision-making, exploring alternative systems, and seeking a diplomatic resolution.
"We have set up committees regarding the contract and the company's compliance failures, another to look for alternatives, and a third to seek a diplomatic solution," he said after launching the ministry's 2026 Jiwa Murni Madani programme at Felda Maokil 1 here today.
He was asked to comment on the recent cancellation of the NSM procurement from Norwegian firm Kongsberg Defence and Aerospace ASA.
Following the move by the Norwegian Defence Ministry, Malaysia protested, labelling the decision unilateral and unacceptable.
Khaled previously said he would meet his Norwegian counterpart in Singapore at the end of this month to seek clarification on the abrupt decision to block the export licences.
He said the meeting would also be used to convey the Malaysian government's regret and surprise over Norway's decision to halt the delivery of the assets just days before the scheduled date in March.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim recently warned that the decision would seriously impact Malaysia's defence operational readiness and the Littoral Combat Ship (LCS) modernisation programme, with wider implications for regional balance.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: First batch of TVET students head to China for AI, engineering training
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Thirty-five students under the Malaysia-China Youth TVET Training (MCYTT) 2.0 programme for 2026 departed for China today to undergo training in Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Technical Mechanical fields.
The students form the first batch of participants under the initiative, marking the official launch of the latest edition of the international Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) mobility programme.
The Malaysian Indian Community Transformation Unit (Mitra), in a statement, said the flag-off ceremony was held at Tune Hotel KLIA Aeropolis here.
The group departed on two separate flights at 4.30pm and 5.40pm today.
The high-impact youth talent development initiative, funded entirely by Mitra under the Human Resources Ministry, received an allocation of RM2.9 million under the 2025 budget.
The programme is organised by the National Skilled Workers Organisation (Belia Mahir) in collaboration with TANG International Education Group, with the Malaysian Indian Youth Council (MIYC) serving as a strategic partner.
Mitra director-general Raveendran Nair expressed pride over the successful implementation of the programme, which has now entered its overseas deployment phase.
"China is now far ahead in the technology and AI sectors. In fact, the electric vehicle (EV) industry there is expanding rapidly.
"I hope the students will make full use of this golden opportunity to gain knowledge, valuable experience and return home with stronger motivation," he said.
Registration for MCYTT 2.0, which ran from Feb 3 to Feb 28, received an overwhelming response with 907 applications submitted.
However, only 500 applicants who met the programme's strict criteria were selected for the international mobility initiative.
In line with efforts to strengthen social mobility, priority was given to active students from public and private higher education institutions as well as skills training institutes from B40 and M40 family backgrounds.
MCYTT 2.0 serves as an international TVET mobility platform aimed at exposing Malaysian youths to advanced technology ecosystems, dynamic work cultures and global skills training systems.
The remaining participants will be sent in stages to gain exposure in several high-technology and strategic sectors, including AI and Technical Mechanical training, EV technology, automation and robotics, as well as modern logistics, automotive engineering and electronics.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Second Dubai' vision not impossible for Perlis, says Raja of Perlis
Article:
ARAU: The Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail, has envisioned the state developing rapidly into a "Second Dubai" in the future.
The ruler said the vision was achievable if the government and the people worked harder together.
He acknowledged that his previous remarks regarding the vision might have seemed too sudden or ambitious for some, but maintained that it remained his long-term goal.
"If we look back 50 years ago, Dubai was just a desert without skyscrapers, but today it is rapidly developing.
"Perlis too can become like Dubai one day. However, it will take time, as the saying goes, 'Rome was not built in a day'. We must set this intention now so future generations can continue the vision until it succeeds," he said at his 83rd birthday celebration at Istana Arau here today.
Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin said opportunities to realise the goal were already falling into place, particularly through the development of the Perlis Inland Port (PIP).
He also expressed pride in the growth of the state's tourism sector, which has successfully transformed Perlis from a transit point into a preferred holiday destination.
"I remember 10 years ago, we were at a loss on how to attract tourists because Perlis was just a transit location to Thailand, while in Kuala Perlis, people merely passed through to get to Langkawi.
"But today, many tourists come specifically to Perlis. We must enhance existing attractions such as our local food and the famous Harumanis mangoes. We need to strengthen our marketing to draw more visitors," he said.
He also recorded his appreciation to the security forces, including the armed forces and the police, for ensuring national peace and security.
"I take this opportunity to thank the firemen who worked tirelessly last month to extinguish the hill fires. They worked day and night, leaving their families behind to fulfil their duties," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Please don't betray us,' Aminuddin tells PH members
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun has described the recent political crisis in the state as an act of betrayal by "friends" who had previously shown loyalty to the Pakatan Harapan (PH) administration.
Aminuddin, who is also Negri Sembilan PH chairman, said although the issue had been resolved, it had left behind the scars of betrayal.
"In Negri Sembilan, we have a large ship. The ship we built is strong and solid, able to withstand storms, and we have travelled a long journey together.
"As captains, they showed loyalty while on the ship, but suddenly they became uncomfortable — perhaps it was too hot, perhaps they wanted something new — so they drilled holes from within.
"The ship was punctured, and water entered. I wondered why it was done, without any explanation, without any signs, without any indication of dissatisfaction. Then I realised that when building the ship, perhaps I forgot to include a swimming pool.
"So when the ship was punctured, it left an impact. Although the holes can be patched, the marks of leakage remain. And those marks are signs of the betrayal that happened before," he said.
He said this while delivering the Negri Sembilan PH representatives' speech at the PH 2026 Convention at the Persada International Convention Centre here today.
Aminuddin said the political developments in Negri Sembilan showed that attention should not only be focused on possible state elections in Johor and Melaka, but also on Negri Sembilan.
He said the PH 2026 Convention 2026 demonstrated that the coalition nationwide was prepared to face elections whenever they are called.
"What happened to us today shows that unity cannot simply be taken for granted. Before the party elections in Negri Sembilan, things were quite chaotic, but today Negri Sembilan is united and strong.
"We do not look at who they are; what matters is that they are within PH. Please do not betray us."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesian couple among 22 migrants fined over immigration offences
Article:
PASIR MAS: An Indonesian couple were among 22 undocumented migrants charged with immigration offences at the Sessions Court here today.
All 22 pleaded guilty before Sessions Court Judge Zulkifli Abdullah.
Thirteen were charged under Section 15(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63 for overstaying, seven under Section 6(1)(c) for entering without valid travel documents, and five under Regulation 39(b) of the Immigration Regulations 1963 for misuse of passes.
The court fined 17 of the accused RM10,000 each, in default three months' imprisonment, while the remaining five were fined RM1,000 each, in default one month's jail.
Some of the accused, speaking through an interpreter, appealed for leniency.
Deputy public prosecutors Izuandi Mat Zain and Nurbahiah Roslee prosecuted, while three migrants were represented by lawyer Wan Amani Wan Azimi.
The 22 were arrested by the Immigration Department during a series of operations in Gua Musang on May 7, targeting a nasi kandar restaurant and a car wash centre.
Malaysia continues to attract foreign workers due to strong demand for labour in sectors such as services, construction and plantations, which has also contributed to a large number of undocumented migrants.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MPS urges government to drop appeal against liquid nicotine ruling
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Pharmacists Society (MPS) has urged the government not to appeal a High Court ruling which found the exemption of liquid and gel nicotine from the Poisons Act 1952 to be irrational and procedurally flawed.
MPS president Amrahi Buang said the government should accept the court's decision to strengthen public health governance and protect the younger generation from nicotine addiction.
He said immediate steps must be taken to ensure liquid and gel nicotine used in vape and electronic cigarettes are placed back under appropriate poison controls.
"We urge the government not to appeal this decision. This should be a turning point. Malaysia must choose prevention over addiction, evidence over convenience, and public health over revenue," he said in a statement.
The High Court here recently allowed a judicial review application filed by three non-governmental organisations (NGOs) to challenge the government's 2023 decision to exempt vape liquids and gels from the Poisons List.
The three NGOs are the Malaysian Council for Tobacco Control (MCTC), the Malaysian Green Lung Association (MGLA), and Voice of the Children (VOC).
The court ruled that the health minister's action at the time to remove liquid nicotine used in vape and electronic cigarettes from the list was irrational and made without proper consultation with the Poisons Board.
Former health minister Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa had said that she had been informed that the Health Ministry would file an appeal against the decision.
Zaliha maintained that the appeal process was crucial to rectify the
legal interpretation
regarding a minister's executive power in making policy decisions for strategic national interests.
Amrahi said MPS welcomed the High Court's ruling, adding that it sent a clear signal that public health protection must not be bypassed for administrative convenience or government revenue.
He said for nearly two years, MPS had consistently raised concerns that removing liquid nicotine from poison controls would create a dangerous regulatory vacuum.
"As pharmacists, our concerns are not abstract. We understand that easier access to nicotine products ultimately impacts the community, especially the youth and healthcare workers who assist in smoking and vaping cessation efforts.
"MPS is a member organisation of MCTC, one of the three NGOs involved in the judicial review. MPS stands with MCTC and the broader public health movement in welcoming this decision," he said.
Amrahi said the court's findings also served as a reminder that proper processes, including expert advice and the role of the Poisons Board, are vital.
"The consultation process cannot be a mere formality after a decision has effectively been made. The Poisons Board is not a rubber-stamp committee because decisions involving scheduled poisons require scientific, professional, and regulatory scrutiny.
"Pharmacists understand this because they enforce the Poisons Act daily through the safe control, supply, counselling, documentation, and monitoring of medicines and poisons," he said, adding that pharmacists face the consequences on the ground when protections under the act are weakened.
Amrahi urged the government to take stricter action against the sale and promotion of vapes targeting youth, expand smoking and vaping cessation support services, and give healthcare professionals a larger role in future nicotine control policies.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Philippines asks top court to allow ICC drug war arrest of senator
Article:
MANILA: The Philippines said it has asked the country's Supreme Court to allow it to arrest ex-president Rodrigo Duterte's chief drug war enforcer to stand trial in an international tribunal.
The International Criminal Court last week unsealed an arrest warrant against Senator Ronald Dela Rosa, accusing him along with Duterte and other "co-perpetrators" of the "crime against humanity of murder."
Dela Rosa briefly sought refuge in the Senate last week while asking the Philippine Supreme Court to stop an ongoing attempt by government agents to arrest him.
"By his own conduct, he has placed himself outside the protection of the law," the government's chief lawyer, Solicitor-General Darlene Berberabe, said in a filing that asked the court to dismiss Dela Rosa's petition.
"Until he submits himself to lawful authorities, he must be deemed a fugitive from justice and should not be allowed to seek any relief from the courts," said the document, dated May 16 but only made available to the media on Sunday.
Dela Rosa's current whereabouts were unknown after he discreetly left the Senate building on Thursday.
The justice department said Friday the ICC warrant would only be served once the Supreme Court resolves Dela Rosa's Supreme Court petition  – unless he tried to flee abroad in which case he would be detained.
The court made no immediate comment.
Dela Rosa has said the ICC warrant was illegal since the Philippines pulled out in 2019 from the treaty that created the tribunal.
Berberabe argued that withdrawal from the treaty "does not relieve a state party from cooperating in proceedings already initiated before the ICC."
The ICC prosecutor launched an examination of the Philippine drug crackdown in 2018.
The bloody campaign, launched by Duterte after being elected president in 2016, left thousands dead, many of them drug users and low-level narcotics peddlers, according to human rights monitors.
Duterte was arrested in March last year, flown to the Netherlands on the same day, and is detained in The Hague awaiting trial.
Dela Rosa served as national police chief from 2016 to 2018.
After retirement, he was elected to the Senate in 2019 and retained his seat in last year's midterm elections.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Johor is a BN govt': Khaled Nordin says no need for talks with federal leaders over solo move
Article:
MUAR: Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) decided to contest all 56 state seats in the upcoming state election because the state administration is not a unity government.
Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said Johor BN and Umno previously held a clear majority, and the decision was made based on the current situation.
He said BN and Umno did not need to discuss the matter with the federal unity government leadership as they anticipated that the federal leaders would not agree with the party's decision to contest all seats.
"All parties must understand that the Johor state government is a BN government. BN had the majority when forming the government.
"That is why this decision to contest all 56 state seats was made by the BN leadership at the state level," he said at a press conference after launching the Defence Ministry's 2026 Jiwa Murni Madani Programme at Felda Maokil 1 here today.
Yesterday, Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said Johor BN announced it would
contest all state seats
after taking into account the views of the Johor people and the voice of grassroots leadership.
The decision was reached after discussions with the party top leadership to form a stable, strong government that serves the state's development and the well-being of Bangsa Johor.
Onn Hafiz said Johor BN remained committed to continuing the state's development agenda and ensuring people's welfare remained a priority.
In the 15th Johor state election in March 2022, BN formed the state government after winning 40 out of 56 state seats.
Pakatan Harapan (PH) won 12 seats, with DAP taking 10, while PKR and Amanah won one seat each.
Perikatan Nasional (PN) won three seats, and Muda secured one.
The Johor state legislative assembly is expected to dissolve automatically on April 21 next year, with the 16th Johor state election required to be held by June 2027 at the latest if the automatic dissolution occurs.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kok Lanas rep to retain state seat despite Bersatu sacking
Article:
KOTA BARU: Kok Lanas assemblyman Datuk Mohamed Farid Mohamed Zawawi will retain his seat despite being sacked from Bersatu, says Kelantan State Legislative Assembly Speaker Datuk Mohd Amar Nik Abdullah.
Amar said Farid's situation differed from the previous Nenggiri state seat vacancy that triggered a by-election.
"Farid was sacked by his party, similar to the cases involving three assemblymen in Perlis and Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin. Under the Constitution, their seats remain secure because they were expelled," he said when contacted.
He said the Nenggiri situation was different because the incumbent's membership was automatically terminated after supporting another party, which legally vacated the seat and forced a by-election.
"Perhaps due to this factor, Bersatu has not sent any official notification to us regarding the Kok Lanas assemblyman's expulsion.
"As of now, we only know through media reports. Even if Bersatu informs us, his position as an assemblyman remains unaffected," Amar said.
Media reports previously stated that Farid's Bersatu membership was terminated effective May 11.
The notice was issued by Bersatu disciplinary board chairman Datuk Mohd Radzi Manan, who said the Kok Lanas assemblyman had violated Clause 9.1.4 of the party constitution and its code of ethics.
The notice added that Farid has 14 days from the date of issuance to appeal the decision.
Commenting on Farid's position as deputy speaker, Amar said the matter rests entirely with the state government.
"The Speaker's Office does not interfere, and the decision depends on the state government. So far, I have not been informed or heard of any changes regarding the deputy speaker post," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Riyadh intensifies awareness on  visiting prophet's mosque etiquettes
Article:
MADINAH: Saudi Arabia's General Presidency for Religious Affairs at the Grand Mosque and the Prophet's Mosque has intensified awareness and guidance efforts to educate pilgrims and visitors on the etiquette of visiting the Prophet's Mosque and the proper manner of greeting the Prophet and his two companions.
According to Saudi Press Agency (SPA), the efforts are being carried out through an integrated system of guidance tools and enriching content presented in several languages, enhancing the religious and intellectual awareness of visitors during the haj season.
The awareness programmes include delivering direct guidance messages within the Prophet's Mosque and its courtyards, in addition to utilising digital screens, awareness publications, and multilingual digital content.
The programmes aim to educate pilgrims on the rulings and etiquette related to the visit and to promote conduct that reflects reverence for the holy site while preserving tranquility and order within the Prophet's Mosque, it added.
The presidency affirmed its commitment to delivering awareness messages through accessible means that take into account the diversity of pilgrims' nationalities and languages as part of efforts to enrich their spiritual experience and promote the moderate message of the Two Holy Mosques.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Couple deny abusing nephew in Kelantan court case
Article:
KOTA BARU: A couple pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court in Machang today to a charge of abusing their 10-year-old nephew last month.
The accused, Wan Saiful Azuan Wan Idris, 31, and Yujas Nor Aida Jasmin Mohd Yusoff, 29, however, denied the charge before Judge Zulkifli Abdullah.
According to the charge, the couple allegedly acted together in abusing the victim, causing injury.
They were alleged to have committed the offence at a house in Kampung Sungai Mas, Machang, at 10pm on April 19.
The pair were charged under Section 31(1)(a) of the Child Act 2001, which provides for a fine of up to RM50,000 or imprisonment of up to 20 years, or both, upon conviction.
Deputy public prosecutor Nur Syafiqah Mohamad prosecuted, while the accused were represented by lawyers Mohd Taufikrullah Rusli and Muhd Zulfaqar Saharrudin.
Nur Syafiqah objected to bail, saying the offence was serious.
However, Taufikrullah appealed for bail, saying it was the couple's first offence and they had no prior criminal record.
The court allowed bail of RM8,000 each with one surety and fixed June 15 for mention and submission of documents.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Vatican gets serious about AI with new commission
Article:
ROME: The Vatican is to establish a commission to address the rapid development of artificial intelligence (AI), reported German news agency dpa.
Pope Leo XIV has approved the creation of the Inter-Dicasterial Commission on Artificial Intelligence to examine the "potential effects on human beings and on humanity as a whole" of AI.
Representatives of several Vatican bodies are to sit on the commission, the Holy See said.
AI is a subject close to the pope's heart. Leo has repeatedly addressed AI — and its potential risks — in speeches and public appearances.
He placed AI on the agenda at the very start of his pontificate in May last year, advocating a healthy alliance between technology and humanity that keeps the risks and dangers of AI in view.
The topic is also expected to feature in Leo's first major doctrinal letter.
According to the official news portal Vatican News, publication of the so-called encyclical is expected towards the end of the month.
Several media reports say the working title of the encyclical is "Magnifica humanitas" ("Magnificent humanity").
The new commission's task is to "facilitate collaboration and the exchange of information amongst the members of the group regarding activities and projects concerning
Artificial Intelligence, including policies on its use within the Holy See, promoting dialogue, communion and participation."
A Vatican body will take on coordination during the commission's first year of work.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Law can't replace love for parents
Article:
LETTERS:
The proposal by Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi to consider introducing legislation compelling children to care for their parents is, in many ways, deeply troubling and unnecessary.
It is sad and rather unbelievable that in a society built upon family values, love, sacrifice, and gratitude, may now require laws to compel children to fulfil what should naturally be a moral duty and responsibility.
Parents devote their lives to raising their children — nurturing them with love, making sacrifices, providing education, protection, and opportunities for them to succeed in life. It should therefore come from the heart, and not from fear of punishment, for children to care for their ageing parents.
While the intention behind such a proposal may stem from growing concerns about neglect and abandonment of elderly parents, legislating love, gratitude, and compassion is not the ideal solution.
Respect and care for parents are values that must be cultivated from a young age through education, family upbringing, moral teachings, and societal values. If society reaches a stage where children need to be forced by law to care for their own parents, then it reflects a deeper erosion of values and humanity that must be addressed at its roots.
It does not speak well of a nation when it has to resort to passing legislation to make the care of elderly parents a responsibility of their children.
What is more important is for schools, communities, religious bodies, and families to continuously instil the importance of filial piety, gratitude, empathy, and responsibility towards parents and elders.
Campaigns promoting family values, stronger moral education, and community awareness programmes can go a long way in strengthening these bonds.
Children must understand that caring for their parents is not a burden, but a meaningful responsibility and a way of honouring those who gave them life and shaped their future.
At the same time, society and the government should also look into strengthening support systems for senior citizens, including healthcare, social protection, emotional support, and accessible elderly care services. A caring society cannot depend solely on legislation, but must be built upon compassion, conscience, and strong family values.
Ultimately, no law can truly replace genuine love and gratitude. The best care for parents comes from the heart, not from compulsion.
TAN SRI LEE LAM THYE
SOCIAL ADVOCATE
The views expressed in this article are the author's own and do not necessarily reflect those of the New Straits Times
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Americans 'should demonstrate like the French,' says Woody Harrelson
Article:
CANNES: US actor Woody Harrelson told AFP that Americans should "demonstrate like the French" because "we let our government get away with so much" as he premiered a new black comedy Saturday at the Cannes Film Festival.
The "Larry Flynt" and "Natural Born Killers" star – who describes himself as "anarchist or probably more a libertarian" – insisted he loved "French people protesting."
"The French are like, no way. It's refreshing. I wish we were more like that," he said.
Harrelson, who famously said that he "felt dirty" after spending an evening with US President Donald Trump two decades ago, said "this whole world's going to hell in a handbasket."
He plays alongside fellow Hollywood star Kristen Stewart in "Full Phil", the latest wacky dark comedy from French director Quentin Dupieux, best known for "Rubber", his cult B-movie absurdist horror about a serial killer car tyre.
Harrelson said that although "I live in a bubble... at least I'm aware that we are in some serious trouble.
"I feel like there's a lot of terrible shit happening and people are just busy on their phones."
Harrelson's famous rant against Trump first surfaced in 2016 at the Toronto Film Festival, and he later said he was so bored with his "brutal" evening with him that he had to go smoke a joint afterwards.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor poll speculation grows on WhatsApp claims of June 24 assembly sitting
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Speculation over a possible Johor snap state election has intensified following viral WhatsApp messages alleging a special state assembly sitting on June 24.
Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) and Umno leaders, when contacted, have cautioned the public against treating the messages as official confirmation amid growing political chatter over a possible dissolution of the state assembly.
Johor BN state liaison secretary Datuk Abdul Halim Suleiman said no official statement has been issued on the matter so far.
"When state elections are in the air, many messages circulate. Do not believe messages spread through WhatsApp or unofficial channels. Only trust official statements," he told the New Straits Times.
The speculation gained traction after a message allegedly calling on all Johor assemblymen to prepare for a special Dewan Undangan Negeri (DUN) sitting on June 24 was widely circulated on WhatsApp.
The alleged sitting is said to pave the way for the dissolution of the Johor state assembly ahead of a fresh state election.
However, no state assemblymen have received any formal notification of such a sitting.
Political speculation in Johor intensified after Menteri Besar and Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced yesterday that BN Johor would contest all 56 state seats in the next state election.
Onn Hafiz said the decision was made after discussions with Johor Umno and BN grassroots leaders.
"After discussions with the party leadership, I state with full responsibility that Johor BN will contest all 56 state seats in the coming state election," he said.
The Johor State Legislative Assembly term expires in April 2027 if it is not dissolved earlier.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rafizi Ramli, Nik Nazmi take over Malaysian United Party
Article:
PETALING JAYA, May 17 — A year after their departure from the Cabinet, PKR’s Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad today announced that they will take over the Malaysian United Party (MUP) as their new political platform.
The announcement was made today at the PJ Performing Arts Centre in a symbolic handover ceremony. MUP’s founder and president, Tan Gin Theam, presented the two PKR stalwarts with a mock party registration certificate to officiate the transition.
MUP or Parti Bersama Malaysia is a little-known, Penang-based political party that was officially registered on December 2, 2016.
Today’s announcement coincides with the Pakatan Harapan convention taking place at the same time in Johor Baru, the first time such an event has been held since 2022.
MORE TO COME

Title: KLIA police nab Chinese national who barged past autogates after burning through travel funds
Article:
SEPANG, May 17 — A woman from China was detained after behaving aggressively while attempting to pass through the international departure gate at Terminal 1 of Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) yesterday.
KLIA Police Chief ACP M. Ravi said in the 1.35 pm incident at International Departure Gate C, the suspect was found passing through the security inspection autogate without a valid ticket and acted aggressively when approached by security personnel for inspection.
“Acting on information received, Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) KLIA auxiliary police detained the woman at 1.45 pm on the same day,” he said in a statement today.
Ravi said initial investigations found that the woman had entered the country on April 30 for a holiday with a friend but did not have enough money to purchase a ticket to return to her home country.
He said she was brought before the Sepang Court today and remanded for one day to facilitate further investigations under Section 7 of the Protected Areas and Protected Places Act 1959, which provides for a fine of up to RM1,000 or imprisonment of up to two years, or both, upon conviction. — Bernama

Title: Civil Defence Force chief calls on personnel to remain adaptive, proactive during South-west Monsoon
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, May 17 — Malaysia Civil Defence Force (APM) personnel have been urged to remain prepared for any eventualities during the current South-west Monsoon phase.
APM chief commissioner Mohammad Abiddin Abd Rahman said they must remain adaptive and proactive by leveraging their training and experience to ensure a swift response when needed.
“I am confident that various preparatory measures have been taken by all APM personnel, including conducting a range of training programmes for members and local communities to reduce disaster risks to lives and property,” he said.
He said this while speaking at the Federal Territories (FT) APM assembly here today, which was also attended by FT Civil Defence director Mohd Rosman Abdullah.
The Southwest Monsoon is reported to have begun on May 14 and is expected to continue until September, following the onset of consistent winds blowing from the southwest towards the region.
Recently, the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) director-general Mohd Hisham Mohd Anip reportedly said that during this period, lower humidity levels typically result in reduced cloud formation in most areas.
This condition will lead to lower rainfall distribution across the country compared to other periods, with more dry days than rainy days.
Meanwhile, Mohammad Abiddin Abd Rahman urged APM personnel to remain resilient and committed in carrying out their responsibilities and daily duties in service of religion, nation and country.
“I also hope that we will continue to enhance our skills and knowledge, while strengthening our integrity and team spirit. We are not merely a rescue force, but also a pillar of national resilience trusted by the people,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Couple claims trial to charge of abusing 10‑year‑old nephew in Kota Bharu
Article:
KOTA BHARU, May 17 — A married couple pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to a charge of abusing their nephew last April.
Wan Saiful Azuan Wan Idris, 31, and Yujas Nor Aida Jasmin Mohd Yusoff, 29, entered the plea after the charge was read out to them before Judge Zulkifli Abllah.
The couple was jointly charged with abusing a 10-year-old boy in a manner likely to cause physical injury at a house in Kampung Sungai Mas, Machang, at about 10pm on April 19.
The charge, framed under Section 31(1)(a) of the Child Act 2001, carries a maximum fine of RM50,000, imprisonment of up to 20 years, or both, upon conviction.
The court allowed bail of RM8,000 for each accused with one surety and fixed June 15 for mention and document submission. — Bernama
* If you suspect child abuse, call the following hotlines for free and confidential support: Talian Kasih at 15999 or WhatsApp 019-2615999 (24/7); Talian BuddyBear at 1800-18-2327(BEAR) (noon-midnight daily); and One Crisis Centre (24/7) Wilayah Persekutuan at 03-26155555 (Kuala Lumpur General Hospital), 03-61454333 (Sungai Buloh Hospital) or 03-83124200 (Putrajaya Hospital).

Title: Anwar congratulates Perlis ruler Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin on 83rd birthday
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 17 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has conveyed his congratulations to the Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail, on the occasion of His Royal Highness’s 83rd birthday today.
In a Facebook post, Anwar prayed that Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin, Raja Perempuan of Perlis, Tuanku Tengku Fauziah Almarhum Tengku Abdul Rashid, and the Perlis royal family would continue to be blessed with Allah SWT’s mercy and guidance, and enjoy good health and well-being.
“Paying the utmost respect and extending heartfelt congratulations on the occasion of the 83rd birthday of His Royal Highness Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail, the Ruler of Perlis.
“Daulat Tuanku,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Pakatan to field candidates in all 56 state seats in upcoming Johor polls after BN move, says Aminolhuda
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, May 17 — Johor Pakatan Harapan (PH) today announced that it will contest in all 56 state constituencies in the upcoming polls, following Barisan Nasional’s (BN) announcement yesterday.
Johor PH chief Aminolhuda Hassan said the decision was made after BN Johor announced that they would contest in all state seats on their own.
He said Johor PH will not back down on this decision and dismissed any cooperation with BN for the state polls.
“Johor BN lost in the 2018 election and it is possible that this can happen again in the upcoming state election,” he said when speaking at the 2026 PH Convention held at the Persada Johor International Convention Centre here today.
Yesterday, Johor BN confirmed it would contest all 56 seats in the coming state election, effectively ruling out any electoral cooperation with PH Perikatan Nasional (PN) or even Muda.
Johor BN chief and state menteri besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi was reported saying that the decision was made after taking into account the views of Johoreans as well as the grassroots leadership of Umno and the state BN.
Aminolhuda, who is Johor Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) chief, also criticised the recent appointment of five non-elected state assemblymen, saying it was for BN to remain in power.
He claimed that Johor BN was afraid of losing the people’s support, especially in the urban areas of the state.
“There are seven state constituencies in Johor with more than 100,000 voters where the state government should have carried out an electoral redelineation.
“If the redelineation is done, the urban areas will immediately see an increase of voters who are largely in favour of PH,” he said.
Aminolhuda pointed out that Johor is still a state strongly influenced by PH as the coalition has secured 14 parliamentary seats out of 26 in the state.
On May 7, the Johor state legislative assembly approved a constitutional amendment Bill on the appointment of five non-elected assemblymen.
The Bill had received a two-thirds vote involving 40 out of 56 assemblymen, all of whom are from the government bloc.

Title: Minister: Defence Literacy Programme launched under Jiwa Murni Programme to boost public awareness on geopolitics
Article:
MUAR, May 17 — The Defence Literacy Programme will be introduced as a new component under the Ministry of Defence’s Jiwa Murni Programme (PJM) this year, said Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin.
He said the programme, run by the Malaysian Institute of Defence and Security (MiDAS), aims to educate the public on geopolitics as part of the Comprehensive Defence (Hanruh) strategy.
“It will help Malaysians understand the geopolitical landscape, threats in the South China Sea, and how our defence preparedness compares with neighbouring countries,” he told reporters.
He spoke after launching the 2026 Kementah Madani Jiwa Murni Programme at Dewan Semai Bakti, Felda Maokil, Labis. Also present was the deputy chief of Army, Lt. Gen. Datuk Mohamad Suria Mohamad Saad.
During the event, Mohamed Khaled presented a cheque for RM6 million for the 2025 Kementah Madani PJM implementation.
The cheque was presented to the chief engineer of the Royal Engineer Regiment, Brig. Gen. Sharuddin Mohd Noh.
He also presented an RM500,000 cheque to Maj. Gen. Datuk Mohd Rashidi Bujai, Islamic Spiritual Division of the Malaysian Armed Forces director general, for the Ilmu dan Muafakat Programme (PIKAT).
Mohamed Khaled added that the RM6 million allocation is meant for 57 PJM projects nationwide: 28 (Melaka), 17 (Johor), four (Kedah), three (Perak), two (Kelantan) and one each in Negeri Sembilan, Sarawak and Terengganu.
This year’s PJM projects focus on upgrading mosques, prayer halls, community halls, schools, sports facilities, cemeteries and infrastructure. — Bernama

Title: Nine MA63 issues resolved in three years under Madani govt, strongest record yet, says Mustapha Sakmud
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, May 17 — The Madani Government has achieved its best record in the nation’s history by resolving nine matters under the Malaysia Agreement 1963 (MA63) within just three years of administration.
Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Sabah and Sarawak) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud said the achievement surpassed earlier administrations, which managed to resolve only four matters over a longer period.
He said 13 out of the 29 MA63 claims have now been resolved through the MA63 Implementation Action Council chaired by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
“We have resolved nine matters within three years together with both state governments, with the participation of the Premier of Sarawak and the Chief Minister of Sabah.
“This involves negotiations among the three key regions that formed Malaysia,” he said when delivering his speech as the Sabah Pakatan Harapan (PH) representative at the PH 2026 Convention held at the Persada Johor International Convention Centre here.
Among the key achievements, he said, were the recognition of the Public Works Department (JKR) and Department of Irrigation and Drainage (JPS) of Sabah and Sarawak as technical departments, amendments to the Inland Revenue Board of Malaysia Act to include representatives from the two regions, as well as efforts to strengthen the judiciary.
Mustapha also highlighted the Madani Government’s commitment to raising the special interim allocation to RM600 million for each region, up from RM125.6 million in 2022.
“Where else can you find a government like this? The Madani Government has not only demonstrated its commitment through words, but has also delivered on its manifesto, including appointing a Deputy Prime Minister from Borneo for the first time in the nation’s history,” he said.
He added that development allocations for Sabah had increased from RM5.2 billion in 2022 to RM6.9 billion under this year’s budget to accelerate critical infrastructure projects such as the Pan Borneo Highway.
Mustapha said the commitment showed that PH and the Unity Government remained committed to protecting the rights of the people of Sabah and Sarawak as outlined in the party constitution and the agreement on the formation of Malaysia. — Bernama

Title: Penang Pakatan-BN ties ‘strong and stable’, says Chow Kon Yeow amid Johor rift
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, May 17 — Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow today affirmed that the political alliance between Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Barisan Nasional (BN) in his state remains “strong and stable,” drawing a sharp contrast with the political developments in Johor.
Speaking at the 2026 PH Convention here today, Chow insisted that Penang has prioritised political stability to safeguard the state’s future for its people, investors, and youth.
“It is very important to ensure that the cooperation between the parties in the government can be strengthened,” he said.
“The people at present want stability, investors want stability, and young people looking for jobs and a future also want stability.”
Chow, who also serves as the Penang PH chairman, explained his governance style, saying that he has opted to make responsible, albeit sometimes unpopular, decisions over populist moves to protect the state’s long-term interests.
“I chose to continue with important policies and decisions in order to maintain the state's financial position and infrastructure development for the future of Penang despite being aware that some decisions may cause dissatisfaction,” he said.
Chow’s remarks come just a day after Johor BN chairman and Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced that his coalition will contest all 56 state seats solo in the next election.
The move effectively ends any potential electoral pact with PH in the state.
Johor is not a coalition government and instead has been governed alone by BN since the 2022 state election.
PH is part of the “balancing force”, the term Johor uses for what is effectively the state’s opposition.

Title: Socso health screening shows 59.2pc of Malaysian workers overweight or obese, 19pc have diabetes, says deputy minister
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, May 17 — A total of 59.2 per cent of workers who underwent the Health Screening Programme organised by the Social Security Organisation (Socso) between 2023 and 2025 were found to be overweight or obese, said Deputy Human Resources Minister Datuk Khairul Firdaus Akbar Khan.
He said the findings also showed that about 19 per cent of them had diabetes, while 17.45 per cent suffered from high blood pressure.
He said the analysis further revealed that about 59.24 per cent of those screened had high cholesterol levels, with the risk profile mainly involving workers aged between 40 and 59.
“We need to realise that the pre-retirement phase is a productive period, but at the same time, it is also a critical period for the risk of non-communicable diseases (NCDs).
“In fact, the Health Ministry has also disclosed that only 14.7 per cent of senior citizens in Malaysia are currently able to achieve healthy ageing,” he said when launching the Socso Run and Ride 2026 programme in conjunction with Workers’ Day 2026 here today.
Also present Human Resources Ministry (KESUMA) deputy secretary-general (Policy and International), who is carrying out the duties of KESUMA secretary-general, Dr Mohd Shaharin Umar; Youth and Sports Ministry secretary-general Datuk Rahimi Ismail; Socso chairman Datuk Seri Subahan Kamal; and Socso Group chief executive officer Datuk Seri Dr Mohammed Azman Aziz Mohammed.
Khairul Firdaus also voiced concern over cancer, which remains the third leading cause of death among Malaysians and is now increasingly affecting younger people.
He urged greater awareness of the dangers of NCDs and advised workers not to take the risks lightly, while encouraging them to adopt a healthy lifestyle and balanced diet.
On the Socso Run and Ride 2026, Khairul Firdaus said the programme had shown positive momentum, with today’s participation rate indicating that participants in their 40s and 50s made up nearly 30 per cent of the total turnout.
“This is a very encouraging development and I hope all workers, regardless of age, will continue to embrace a healthy lifestyle.
“This is because workers’ health is a key determinant of our productivity and indirectly influences socio-economic sustainability,” he said.
The ninth edition of the Socso Run and Ride 2026 recorded its largest participation to date, with 12,000 participants joining the programme, reflecting KESUMA’s support in driving collective lifestyle changes. — Bernama

Title: Pakatan convention to chart new strategies for nation’s future, says Fahmi
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, May 17 — The Pakatan Harapan (PH) 2026 Convention held today serves as the best platform for component parties of the coalition to formulate new strategies and approaches to ensure a brighter future for the country, said Datuk Fahmi Fadzil.
The PH 2026 Convention director said the convention, which is being held again after four years, provides delegates with the opportunity to pool ideas and energy for the benefit of Malaysians.
Fahmi, who is also Communications Minister, said the convention is also an important platform to assess the achievements of the Madani Government, which is now yielding positive results that cannot be disputed by any party.
“This convention is the best opportunity for us to gather our strength, ideas, and present proposals, formulas and approaches that we need to pursue to ensure a better future for Malaysia,” he said.
He said this in his welcoming speech at the PH 2026 Convention at the Persada Johor International Convention Centre here.
Fahmi also stressed that the focus of this year’s convention includes preparations for the Melaka and Johor state elections, which are expected to be held in the near future.
